subject: Falsifiability rehearsal — cross-tracker fallback contract

rehearsals.mutation: Replaced the body of `proposeFallback` with `return null` (deleted the existing if/return logic).

rehearsals.post_revert_run: BUILD SUCCESSFUL — all 6 CrossTrackerFallbackPolicyTest cases pass on the restored implementation.

rehearsals.mutation: Replaced the body of `proposeFallback` with `return null` (i.e. cross-tracker layer disabled inside the SDK).

rehearsals.reverted: git checkout -- core/tracker/client/src/main/kotlin/lava/tracker/client/CrossTrackerFallbackPolicy.kt (verified: git diff returns empty)

rule_under_test: Sixth Law clause 6.6.2 + Seventh Law clause 1 — every test in this codebase MUST be falsifiable by deliberately breaking the production code path it claims to verify. Each rehearsal above demonstrates that the named test fails with a clear assertion message when the corresponding implementation is mutated, and passes again on revert.
notes: Rehearsal-B is the load-bearing one for the SP-3a Task 4.8 contract: it confirms that the SearchOutcome.CrossTrackerFallbackProposed emission depends mechanically on CrossTrackerFallbackPolicy.proposeFallback returning a non-null value. Removing that dependency surface causes the outcome to collapse to Failure (the legacy pre-Phase-4 shape), and the test's primary assertion fires on that collapse.
